items.title: insane how well KAT played in the Knicks' run to a title.
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
insane how well KAT played in the Knicks' run to a title.

He averaged 15.9 points (on 55% shooting), 10.6 rebounds, 4.9 assists, 1.3 steals and 1.3 blocks.

Per @basketball-reference.com 
No other player in NBA history ever matched or exceeded those numbers in a championship postseason.

items.title: A Statement from the desk of T. Zilly
items.author: Tom Ziller
items.body_excerpt: A Statement from the desk of T. Zilly

images.alt:
A Statement on Coincidental Colonoscopy References
In Thursday's newsletter, I referred to Boston's Jaylen Brown trade as "a fandom-level jug of colonoscopy prep." I had no information at that time suggesting that Bill Simmons, one of the most prominent Celtics fans in media, was undergoing a colonoscopy at the moment the Jaylen Brown trade news broke, a story he told with great relish on his Thursday podcast.

I have no inside knowledge of Bill Simmons' medical appointments, and the metaphorical reference to colonoscopy prep in the newsletter was a hilarious coincidence.

items.title: Neemias Queta last season: 10.2 PPG, 8.4 RPG, 1.3 BPG
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Neemias Queta last season: 10.2 PPG, 8.4 RPG, 1.3 BPG

5th highest net rating in the NBA
5th in win shares per 48
3rd highest FG% in the NBA
3rd in EFG%
4th in TS%
8th in offensive rebounds
11th in rebounds
2nd in screen assists

He seized his opportunity and now gets contractually rewarded for it.

media.title: Last stop Hurricanes, next stop Heat for well-schooled Donaldson at summer league
media.description: The learning curve can be unforgiving for undrafted NBA prospects, Or you could arrive as a veteran, a well-schooled collegiate veteran, like Tre Donaldson with the Miami Heat.

items.title: Hollander POTD Horace Grant. Clemson PF was drafted 10th by CHI in 87. Took starting job in 88. Super quick defender & was huge in CHI's swarming defense in the early 90s. Also played w/ORL, SEA & LAL (won 4th ring w/LA in 01). Never had huge stats but was great player.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollander POTD Horace Grant. Clemson PF was drafted 10th by CHI in 87. Took starting job in 88. Super quick defender & was huge in CHI's swarming defense in the early 90s. Also played w/ORL, SEA & LAL (won 4th ring w/LA in 01). Never had huge stats but was great player.

items.title: I don’t think the Knicks should try to “replace” Mitchell Robinson. that’s faulty Breakup Theory. we’d spend the whole season being like “ugggh Mitch would have gotten that rebound i miss him.” Get a big soft dork who can shoot and pass so it feels like you were missing something
